evidence.evidence_text: International Journal of Nanomedicine Dovepress openaccesstoscientificandmedicalresearch OpenAccessFullTextArticle ORIGINAL RESEARCH Green Synthesis, Characterization and Antimicrobial Activity of Copper Oxide Nanomaterial Derived from Momordica charantia ThisarticlewaspublishedinthefollowingDovePressjournal: InternationalJournalofNanomedicine Hina Qamar 1 Background:In the emerging field of nanotechnology, copper oxide (CuO) nanomaterials Sumbul Rehman 2 are considered to be one of the most important transition metal oxides owing to its DushyantKumarChauhan 1 fascinating properties.

Advia(UnaniPharmacology),A.K.Tibbiya Materials and Methods: To synthesize CuO NRs, 0.1M of CuSO .5H O solution was 4 2 College,AligarhMuslimUniversity, addedto aqueousextractofMomordicacharantia ina1:3 (v/v)ratio(pH=11)andheated Aligarh,UttarPradesh,India;3Biological StandardizationDivision,Indian at 50°C followed by washing and drying.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: Advia(UnaniPharmacology),A.K.Tibbiya Materials and Methods: To synthesize CuO NRs, 0.1M of CuSO .5H O solution was 4 2 College,AligarhMuslimUniversity, addedto aqueousextractofMomordicacharantia ina1:3 (v/v)ratio(pH=11)andheated Aligarh,UttarPradesh,India;3Biological StandardizationDivision,Indian at 50°C followed by washing and drying. The synthesized CuO NRs were subjected to VeterinaryResearchInstitute,Bareilly, characterization using different analytical techniques such as UV visible spectroscopy, UttarPradesh,India zeta sizer equipped with zeta potential, Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), X-ray diffraction (XRD), scanning electron microscopy (SEM) equipped with energy- dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM).

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: erization using different analytical techniques such as UV visible spectroscopy, UttarPradesh,India zeta sizer equipped with zeta potential, Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR), X-ray diffraction (XRD), scanning electron microscopy (SEM) equipped with energy- dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM). Further, the application as a biomedical therapeutic potential was evaluated in vitro using well diffusion method against eleven multidrug-resistant clinical bacterial strains, afungus-TrichophytonrubrumandinovoagainsttheR Bvirususinghaemagglutination 2 (HA) test.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: Results:Characterization was preliminarily done by the spectral study that confirms the absorbance band at 245nm. FTIR analysis at 628 cm−1 peak identified copper oxide vibration.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: SEM analysis revealed agglomerated particle clusters. However, with TEM clear nanorods of average diameter of 61.48 ± 2 nm were observed.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: However, with TEM clear nanorods of average diameter of 61.48 ± 2 nm were observed. EDAX confirmed CuO formation while XRD showed a typical monoclinic structure with 6 nm crystallite size.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: EDAX confirmed CuO formation while XRD showed a typical monoclinic structure with 6 nm crystallite size. Biological screening of CuO NRs showed significant results against both in vitro and in ovo methods.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: Biological screening of CuO NRs showed significant results against both in vitro and in ovo methods. Significant inhibitory activity (p<0.0001) was noted against most of the resistant human pathogenic strains including both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: Significant inhibitory activity (p<0.0001) was noted against most of the resistant human pathogenic strains including both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. The highest efficacy was observed against Bacillus cereus with a 31.66 mm zone of inhibition.

record.title: CharanSinghUniversity,Meerut250001, Keywords: copper oxide nanorods, CuO NRs, biosynthesis, antibacterial, antifungal,

evidence.evidence_text: The highest efficacy was observed against Bacillus cereus with a 31.66 mm zone of inhibition. Besides, the therapeutic potential of CuO NRs against Corynebacterium xerosis, Streptococcus viridians and R B strain of Newcastle disease 2 is reported for the first time.
